jokes.text: Frank waking up confused and disoriented, clearly hungover and not remembering where he is
jokes.setup: Opening scene with Frank waking up
jokes.punchline: His complete confusion about basic location and state
jokes.explanation: Establishes Frank's state and the episode's POV effectively, solid character moment

jokes.text: Frank desperately searching for toilet while drunk/hungover
jokes.setup: Frank needs to urinate urgently
jokes.punchline: Frantic search and relief at finding toilet
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy, functional but unoriginal

jokes.text: Frank looking in mirror and saying 'Still got it' while clearly disheveled
jokes.setup: Frank examining himself in mirror after rough night
jokes.punchline: His delusional self-confidence despite obvious state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank delusion - thinking he looks good when he clearly doesn't

jokes.text: Frank pretending he knows what's happening when he clearly doesn't
jokes.setup: Dennis asking if Frank knows what's happening
jokes.punchline: Frank's transparent attempt to bluff knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank move - confidently lying when caught off guard, very in-character

jokes.text: Frank trying to remember Dennis's name: 'Is it Damon? No. Delroy. No. DeMarcus.'
jokes.setup: Frank trying to recall Dennis's name
jokes.punchline: The increasingly wrong and inappropriate guesses
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work - shows Frank's deteriorating mental state while being unexpectedly problematic. Good escalation of wrongness.

jokes.text: Frank deflecting with 'Shut up, bird!' when pressured
jokes.setup: Frank being asked to explain the plan he doesn't know
jokes.punchline: Using his go-to Dee insult as deflection
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character consistency, uses established running gag strategically

jokes.text: Frank misreading 'LOL' as 'LOM' and getting confused by texting
jokes.setup: Frank trying to text with Pondy
jokes.punchline: His confusion with basic text abbreviations
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frank's age/tech illiteracy but fairly predictable

jokes.text: Frank assuming break-in is 'Probably Asians' with no context
jokes.setup: Frank hearing a noise at his apartment
jokes.punchline: Immediate racist assumption
jokes.explanation: Dark character work showing Frank's casual prejudice, but relies on shock value more than craft.

jokes.text: Landlord eating snake meat sandwich as intimidation
jokes.setup: Landlord trying to intimidate Frank about rent
jokes.punchline: His choice of snake meat as power move
jokes.explanation: Weird enough to be memorable, sets up the choking payoff

jokes.text: 'Is that what you people eat?' - Frank's racist response to snake meat
jokes.setup: Learning about snake meat sandwich
jokes.punchline: Frank's immediate racist categorization
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Frank's character but predictable shock humor

jokes.text: Frank wishing death on landlord, then landlord actually starts choking
jokes.setup: Frank saying 'I hope you choke on that, and you die'
jokes.punchline: Landlord immediately starts choking for real
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark irony - Frank's death wish immediately comes true, great structure

jokes.text: Frank eating the snake meat while landlord is dying and also starting to choke
jokes.setup: Landlord choking on snake meat
jokes.punchline: Frank deciding to try it himself and also choking
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Frank's selfishness and stupidity - even while watching someone die from food, he tries it

jokes.text: 'Somebody throw me in the trash.'
jokes.setup: Frank thinking he's dying from choking
jokes.punchline: His final request is to be thrown in trash
jokes.explanation: Iconic Frank line - perfectly encapsulates his low self-worth and becomes highly quotable meme

jokes.text: Frank waking up in hospital thinking he died and went to heaven because of nurse's breasts
jokes.setup: Frank waking up confused about where he is
jokes.punchline: Thinking hospital is heaven due to attractive nurse
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank horniness, consistent character moment

jokes.text: Frank completely ignoring doctor's cancer diagnosis to stare at nurse's 'Winnebagos'
jokes.setup: Doctor delivering serious medical news
jokes.punchline: Frank's total distraction by sexual thoughts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank behavior - even terminal diagnosis can't override his libido. 'Winnebagos' is great euphemism

jokes.text: 'Dumb bird got a boot on her car'
jokes.setup: Frank seeing Dee's car situation
jokes.punchline: His unsympathetic 'bird' comment
jokes.explanation: Consistent callback usage, economical

jokes.text: Mac explaining he's 'too strong' after throwing rug completely over fence instead of draping it
jokes.setup: Mac failing simple task of draping rug on fence
jokes.punchline: His excuse that he's too strong/powerful
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac delusion - turning obvious failure into proof of his superiority

jokes.text: Mac asking Frank if Dennis hates him and wanting Dennis to think he's cool
jokes.setup: Mac opening up to Frank about insecurities
jokes.punchline: His desperate need for Dennis's approval
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mac's pathetic neediness

jokes.text: Mac asking Frank to mention his bench press stats to Dennis while Frank eats dog treats
jokes.setup: Mac trying to get Frank to boost his reputation
jokes.punchline: Frank completely ignoring him to eat drugged dog treats
jokes.explanation: Great layering - Mac's insecurity vs Frank's drug-driven priorities

jokes.text: 'Whoa, Jews! What is this?'
jokes.setup: Frank realizing he's at a Jewish gathering
jokes.punchline: His tactless exclamation
jokes.explanation: Frank's casual bigotry, in-character but relies on shock value

jokes.text: 'Uh, uh, excuse me, I'm full of dog poison.'
jokes.setup: Frank trying to say something smart when introduced to family
jokes.punchline: His completely inappropriate confession about the dog treats
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Frank line - his attempt at polite conversation is hilariously inappropriate and honest

jokes.text: Frank finding and crushing pills in medicine cabinet while high
jokes.setup: Frank needing uppers to counteract the downers
jokes.punchline: His drug-addled solution of raiding medicine cabinet
jokes.explanation: Escalates the drug storyline logically but not particularly clever

jokes.text: 'It's Frankenstein!' as Frank bursts in hyped up on stimulants
jokes.setup: Frank high on uppers after taking downers
jokes.punchline: Embracing the Frankenstein nickname from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback usage and fits Frank's manic state

jokes.text: 'Get out of the way, Jew' as Frank grabs the rug
jokes.setup: Frank trying to get the rug in his manic state
jokes.punchline: His casual anti-Semitism while committing theft
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Frank's bigotry but lazy shock humor

jokes.text: 'I got handsy with some pretty young thing. She looked 18 to me.'
jokes.setup: Pondy explaining why he's in trouble
jokes.punchline: His creepy admission with weak excuse
jokes.explanation: Shows Pondy's depravity, consistent with character but predictable

jokes.text: Pondy pulling out gun saying 'Someone's dying today, baby' then Frank talking him down
jokes.setup: Pondy being cornered and desperate
jokes.punchline: Sudden escalation to lethal violence then immediate de-escalation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and de-escalation. Shows both characters' instability and Frank's surprising humanity.

jokes.text: 'Nice nips, cupcake' - Frank's attempt at tough talk
jokes.setup: Frank trying to think of something tough to say
jokes.punchline: His hilariously inappropriate and unthreatening comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Frank line - his idea of intimidation is weirdly sexual and completely ineffective

jokes.text: 'Eat glass, you mangy mutt' to the guard dog
jokes.setup: Frank dealing with guard dog threat
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top hostile response to animal
jokes.explanation: Good Frank aggression, economical and in-character

jokes.text: 'Anybody need a ride?' as Frank's attempted cool line
jokes.setup: Frank trying to think of something cool to say after successful rescue
jokes.punchline: His mundane, practical offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax - Frank's idea of cool is completely ordinary

jokes.text: 'Dee's a bird! She is a bird!' as Frank's deflection
jokes.setup: Mac accidentally touching Frank and Frank panicking
jokes.punchline: Using the bird insult as emergency distraction
jokes.explanation: Strategic callback usage, Frank's panic response is in-character

jokes.text: Sexual innuendo about Frank 'getting in there deep'
jokes.setup: Charlie asking if Frank went deep into something
jokes.punchline: The sexual interpretation of the innocent question
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual double entendre, nothing special

jokes.text: Frank pretending to know about tomorrow's plans: 'We got the thing with the thing.'
jokes.setup: Charlie testing if Frank knows tomorrow's plans
jokes.punchline: Frank's vague non-answer that reveals he has no clue
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Frank's pattern of bluffing, 'thing with the thing' is perfectly vague

jokes.text: Charlie suggesting 'night crawlers' game
jokes.setup: Charlie suggesting alternative to sleep
jokes.punchline: His weird, undefined game proposal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie weirdness - mysterious game that sounds both innocent and disturbing

jokes.text: 'Darkness falls. And magic stirs. As we become... the creatures of the night'
jokes.setup: Charlie setting up his night crawlers game
jokes.punchline: His overly dramatic narration for whatever weird game this is
jokes.explanation: Perfectly unsettling Charlie moment - grandiose introduction to unknown activity leaves audience uncomfortable
